questions: Is the version correctly set to 1.3.3 in both package.json and package-lock.json?
Are there any stale references to version 1.3.2 remaining in source or config files?
Do quality gates (typecheck, build) still pass after the version bump?
Were any unrelated files modified beyond the version bump scope?

answers.answer: Yes. package.json line 3 declares "version": "1.3.3" and package-lock.json lines 3 and 9 both declare "version": "1.3.3". Independently verified via node -p and direct file read.
No. A repo-wide grep for '1.3.2' returned only .helix/ ticket artifacts, which are not part of the publishable codebase. No source, config, or workflow files contain the old version.
evidence: grep for 1.3.2 returned 33 files, all under .helix/tickets/ directory

answers.answer: Yes. Both npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit) and npm run build (tsc) exited with code 0 and produced no errors.
evidence: npm run typecheck: exit 0, no errors
npm run build: exit 0, no errors

answers.answer: No. Only package.json and package-lock.json were modified, both with version-only changes from 1.3.2 to 1.3.3.
evidence: Implementation reported exactly two files changed
Independent review confirms only version fields changed in both files

statement: Code review independently verified all acceptance criteria for BLD-435. Version 1.3.3 is correctly set in package.json and package-lock.json. No stale version references remain. Typecheck and build both pass. No issues found; no code fixes needed. Implementation is correct and ready for verification.
